name: Jimmy Scott
abstract: James Victor Scott (July 17, 1925 – June 12, 2014), also known as Little Jimmy Scott, was an American jazz vocalist known for his high natural contralto voice and his sensitivity on ballads and love songs. After success in the 1940s and 1950s, Scott's career faltered in the early 1960s.

interviews.title: Scott Jimmy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Like Little Jimmy Scott hired me for – to play with him in Brooklyn when he was working in a club there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Diblasio
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Bill Evans play in Philly at it used to be the old Showboat, it was called the Bijou, Bill Evans doing a ballad, Jimmy Scott singing a ballad, McCoy Tyner and Elvin Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The sound is Little Jimmy Scott. The phrasing and all that’s Little Jimmy Scott, to me. I heard – I loved him. I loved to hear Jimmy sing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I think KBCA has one of his records. I've asked and asked, and nobody's ever heard of Jimmy Scott out here, other than that one record.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: the most requested record in my store in its history was Little Jimmy Scott “Falling in Love is Wonderful.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe and Christian Goodwillie, via Zoom, June 30, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I heard Little Jimmy Scott with – Lionel Hampton, wasn’t it? So I had to be maybe 10, then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
